root_motivation: The federated self has four facets (attest, roster, converge, arbiter) but no single mirror that answers 'what am I, right now, as a whole' — corpus-introspect provides that unified self-portrait so the self-review loop and a human operator can understand the entire entity in one glance, from any node.
user_persona: Joe Yen, running self-review on wintermute (single laptop now, multi-node fleet later); the command is also consumed by the self-review playbook via --format selfreview.

acceptance_criteria.description: Given fixture shims for attest/roster/converge/arbiter/tether on PATH returning known JSON, `corpus introspect --json` emits a WholeSelf record whose nodes, leases, and converged fields correctly reflect the fixtures (golden-compared).

The text view renders, for the multi-node fixture, a self-portrait naming each node, its attested+link status, its session count, and the converged/lagging verdict on a bounded number of lines (snapshot-tested).

Graceful degradation: when one upstream shim is absent (e.g. no wm-tether on PATH), that facet is reported as degraded/unknown with a reason and the command still exits success; the missing facet is never silently dropped.

When NO corpus components are installed/configured, `corpus introspect` reports a single-node self (just this box, unattested, no fleet) honestly, rather than erroring — the lone-laptop case reads correctly.
`--format selfreview` emits a parseable block containing the node count, converged verdict, and held-lease count, suitable for the self-review playbook (structural assert).
(deferred — embedded NATS / live fleet) Run on a real two-node fleet, `corpus introspect` lists both nodes with live link RTT and real session counts.
`cargo test` green; `sigpipe::reset()` first in `main()` (grep-asserted); `corpus introspect | head` does not panic (no SIGPIPE).

scope: Synthesize output of corpus-attest, muster --fleet, corpus-converge, corpus-arbiter, wm-tether into a WholeSelf JSON record
Render a human-readable self-portrait (default text view)
Emit --format selfreview block for the self-review playbook
Degrade gracefully when any upstream CLI is missing or errors
Report an honest lone-laptop view when no fleet is present
non_goals: Does NOT implement any corpus facet itself (attest, roster, converge, arbiter are separate crates)
Does NOT manage NATS connections or fleet topology (reads existing CLI surfaces only)
Does NOT persist state — every run is a fresh query of the upstream CLIs
Does NOT provide a daemon or watch mode (one-shot query only in v1)
Does NOT authenticate or encrypt fleet communication (delegates to upstream tools)

additional.sigpipe_reset_required: sigpipe::reset() must be first call in main()

five_whys_trace.q: Why does wintermute need a corpus-introspect command?
five_whys_trace.a: Because the four corpus facets (attest, roster, converge, arbiter) each answer a slice of 'what am I' but there is no single place to see the whole.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does the self-review loop need the whole picture in one place?
five_whys_trace.a: Because the self-review playbook runs on whatever node the operator is on, and assembling a multi-facet picture manually (four commands, possibly two machines) is fragile and error-prone.

five_whys_trace.q: Why is fragility in self-review especially bad here?
five_whys_trace.a: Because self-review is the mechanism by which the entity detects degraded state and queues remediation — if the review itself is incomplete, degraded nodes are invisible and the entity cannot heal.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should the command be `corpus-introspect` (binary) or `corpus introspect` (subcommand)?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Binary is `corpus-introspect`; clap exposes it as `corpus introspect` via an alias or the crate name is corpus-introspect. The PRD says 'corpus introspect [--json]' — implemented as a single binary named corpus-introspect.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What JSON do the upstream fixture shims return for AC1 golden test?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Tests include inline fixture data; the binary invokes upstream CLIs by name, so tests put shim scripts on PATH using a temp dir prepended to PATH.
